I was not asked my intention.I don’t think you should be making the calls on timing out and moderating what people say if you don’t ask what I mean first. Butch simply means masculine. I also was enjoying the server before all this happened, so it’s a real bummer.This escalated even more when you guys started to bring the Grim situation here. Please keep it out of here since it doesn't concern y'all & it can create more problemsI didn’t mention grim, that was the rest of them. I was just supporting them since I found it important to talk about serious topics. If it’s not explained why someone is muted for 3 days, then people get the wrong idea. Being so indirect actually creates more controversy than just telling the truth.I honestly don’t know grim well, but he was the first person I VCed with in the server and was very fun to talk to. I think we are all just confused and want an honest explanation.I can't give y'all any explanations or details on what is happening with him, so I'd appreciate it if you could keep any topics regarding his situation in the text channelsI wasn’t planning on bringing it up again, but I have no power over anyone else but myself. I have one last question before the ticket is closed,if that’s alright. Is there anyone else I can talk to about the situation since you aren’t able to? I just don’t find it fair that someone is barred from a community they enjoyed without explicit explanation. That doesn’t mean I’ll start a riot about it, I really just want information.Sure, I'll have an Administrator reach backAlright, I appreciate it. Please next time keep in mind that some terms might be unfamiliar, and you might want to search it up before timing someone out. Or ask for clarification. Thank you for answering.Am I allowed to still join shifts and speak in the server?YesAlright, just wanted to check so there were no further issues.why was i warned??? we just spoke about the misunderstandingi feel like this is just being dragged on, im not starting drama and we spoke about what happened, this is very unnecessarywhy was i warned??? we just spoke about the misunderstandingHello, I'm sorry I didn't see that the ticket was opened. I have limited time to catch up on issues and support Robot, so from what I read in general, it warranted a warning, and I didn't make it into every moderator ticket.

He asked you to change topics, you refused. Then things became dramatic when everyone wanted to chime in on your sensitive conversation and conflate it with one member's current administrative situation.

Say this is a judicial system, and moderators are police. When they ask you to change topics, that's like a lawful order to stop heading in a direction that might break a rule. If you don't understand why, it's not the place to start a debate or dig deeper for understanding. Instead, you step aside and talk one on one, or one on team with staff to get your clarity, so that 🐸﹒general can continue to be about stress-free light-hearted topics and not a debate about rules or insinuations about someone's character. And if you do not comply with the "lawful order" to cease and desist, you may receive consequences like getting muted, warned, or even kicked.

With Grim, being muted is like being arrested. It is nobody's business but Grim's and the administrative team why he's "under arrest." Since he was a staff member, there's many channels he could have been muted for that you all on the general chat cannot access. You assume that because we didn't explain it to you or to the others chatting at the time that we didn't explain it to anybody, but that is never the case unless they are a hacked account or bot that needs no explanation. You, nor his friends are owed anything until the end of his trial. When a valued member of our community breaks a rule that results in a ban, we do sometimes address his friends, but his friends do not need to be involved in the situation before it is resolved.-
All that being said, since a ticket was eventually opened, I can discuss with administration about removing your warning, but from my perspective, a warning was owed about 20 minutes before the ticket was opened, and you weren't the one who opened it, which tells me you didn't understand the severity of ignoring an administrator's request not to drag on in general about a topic he deemed unfit.

And so there is no confusion, "drag on" is in reference to demanding a public explanation of his reasoning to request you to change topics or open a ticket to learn details as to why.Hello, I'm sorry I didn't see that the ticket was opened. I have limited time to catch up on issues and support Robot, so from what I read in general, it warranted a warning, and I...I understand what you mean, in a way. However, treating a server like a dictatorship will result in a downfall. The reason I wanted it answered publicly, is because it felt shady. It felt discriminatory and homophobic. Do you understand how someone saying “let’s handle this privately” can be seen as a coverup?If you all are police, then why is peaceful protest not allowed? This analogy does not resonate with me.
The reason I was also supporting Grim and his friends is that you guys havent spoken to him. For over a day now.I honestly understand it isn’t my business since I’m not involved with staff, so I’m not interested in bothering with that. All I care about is my friend, as a person, being muted and not communicated to whatsoever. Swiping your problems under the rug is never a good idea. You have to address them and communicate eventually.Also, my personal values believe that it IS the place to stand up for what’s right. If I felt like my identity was being hidden, or discriminated against, I have every right to ask for details. Also, it was the way Robot phrased it. It was blunt and honestly disrespectful. If I was responded to with a lighter tone, I would have made a ticket earlier.Maybe, for future reference, a “I’m sorry you feel that way, I can explain it to you privately” would be more effective and helpful than his vague responses that made me incredibly confused.Also, my personal values believe that it IS the place to stand up for what’s right. If I felt like my identity was being hidden, or discriminated against, I have every right to ask...Re: Dictatorship
We operate this as a democracy with 📬﹒suggestions rhe public votes on and voting among staff in which the owner's vote weighs the same as all staff, but this is ultimately a volunteer-run hobby server and not a life-altering government and any stressful situation may be handled with mutes and bans. Grim has been breaking rules in staff servers and is now suffering the consequences. We do not have to condone riots or protests and cave into peer pressure if the demands go against our Core Values.-
Being that Roblox is designed for players of all ages, we don't really lean into talking about sexuality, even if Discord is for post-pubescent teens who may be exploring their sexuality. I'm an adult, and it makes me uncomfortable to get too much into people's sexual preferences. You're allowed to like who you like, but I don't want jokes related to sexuality in a server I created. I'll still allow it in 🐸﹒deeper-discussion if you want to connect with other LGBTQ+ allies about that particular part of your identity, but sexuality has become politicized, so we steer away from it as a Bloxburg-focused community. There's no sex or dating in Bloxburg, so who you like to date or engage in sexual/romantic activity with has no legitimate place in a server about working in Bloxburg.

I stand with Robot's decision to steer the conversation in a new direction after the "butch lesbian joke" as butch, while the denotation may be harmless, has been used in a derogatory manner against women who don't fit into the stereotypical female norms.

"Open a ticket" is essentially the same thing as, "I can explain it to you privately," so I'm not sure you would have reacted differently seeing as you replied, "I would rather it be said publicly," and I don't know how moving a sensitive and politicized topic into a ticket equates to sweeping it under a rug, when we still have to answer to you in the ticket. Nonetheless, we will certainly review our moderator protocol to ensure these instances are handled better.- Being that Roblox is designed for players of all ages, we don't really lean into talking about sexuality, even if Discord is for post-pubescent teens who may be exploring their s...I’m uncomfortable with how you’re speaking about my community. I’m open about being a lesbian because it’s part of my identity, and always has been. I will never hide who I am. Plus, I’m not interested in dating in a random server- I have a whole girlfriend. Lesbianism is more than just sexual orientation. Also, I don’t need someone telling me about what Butch means. I know many butches in my life who use the term confidently to reclaim it.Sexuality is also not inappropriate. Obviously talking about anything NSFW is- and I would never do that. But implying that me being openly lesbian is inappropriate to people younger is incredibly offensive.Re: Dictatorship We operate this as a democracy with 📬﹒suggestions rhe public votes on and voting among staff in which the owner's vote weighs the same as all staff, but t...I am simply commenting on how the staff have so much power, and don’t listen to its members whenever it comes to important issues.I believe that you and your team need more work and research on LGBT topics. As someone whose been part of the community for a long time, I’m trying to say this- there will be others that are like me, and if it’s handled the same as this was, it will go badly. I’m not trying to be a rude. But whenever people like you treat us like completely different people and expect us to accept that, it really hurts. It makes us feel inhuman. I’m sure you know of the couple on staff that flirt all the time publicly. What about that? How come that’s allowed, but me simply saying the word lesbian is not? If the problem is with romance, then why does it only apply to me?Not to mention my intention was not even romantic… I said two things and apparently that warranted all this.I am simply commenting on how the staff have so much power, and don’t listen to its members whenever it comes to important issues.Would you please clarify what was an important issue we didn't listen to.Would you please clarify what was an important issue we didn't listen to.The whole grim thing- multiple people were asking for answers and they were completely ignored.
It’s important because youre silencing people. I know what Grim did, but talking negatively about things about the server isnt a crime, because it was in DIRECT MESSAGES. It was not public, like how you say is the problem in my case.
It’s just so confusing how theres hypocrisy in your supposed rules and double standards. It’s tiring to try to figure out what your problem is with us.
Also, the fact you ignored the rest of my messages in your response really says something. There was no “Sorry for the misunderstanding.” or “Alright, we’ll work on that.”
It’s okay to make mistakes….. it’s okay to have humility or take accountability. But the only thing you acknowledged in my messages was something that hurt the servers ego.The whole grim thing- multiple people were asking for answers and they were completely ignored. It’s important because youre silencing people. I know what Grim did, but talking ne...As I've said, there have been things going on in staff channels, which is still inside the BEE Hive, but I can understand how he and his friends may have misunderstood. And again, we've not reached a resolution, so we've not determined an official statement.

I'm sorry you view it as ignoring, although I saw an Administrator replying to the messages, so I wouldn't categorize it as ignoring; refusing to accept a request is not the same thing. We just find that discussing a member's rule-breaking action and punishment to everyone often does more harm than good. Not everyone who wants to chat in 🐸﹒general wants to be updated about people who broke rules, so we reserve those details to tickets for the curious.

Also, I've not ignored the rest of your statement; I just wanted to be sure my first question was clarified first. I have the rest of my reply in draft. Our responses are getting quite lengthy, so I don't want the discussion to get muddied by replying to five talking points at once. (I also don't have Nitro, so I'm limited in how long my messages can be)I believe that you and your team need more work and research on LGBT topics. As someone whose been part of the community for a long time, I’m trying to say this- there will be othe...As far as I can tell (as of last night when I wrote this), you're the only one who used a term typically related to someone's identity as a joke. You are not the only non-straight member of our community, and we know you all won't be the last, and we're not afraid of that. However, we don't allow people to joke with terms like bitch, faggot, dyke, including reclaimed racial terms like nigga, so we put butch in that group. This reddit comment aligns with my experience with the word. While I am not a masculine woman who has reclaimed the word, I am a part of a couple of communities who claims a word originally weaponized against them. Not everyone from these communities accept their redefinition, so we don't allow it here. My intention was not to define butch to you, but to explain why it is considered inappropriate for a Roblox-centered community.


I am not aware of a couple flirting with each other here. As I've said, my time on Discord has become tightened, so I mostly deal with reports, and the Grim situation brought your situation to my attention as well. If it makes you uncomfortable, you should definitely report it. I will find time to conduct searches or staff interviews to get more information because I don't think a Roblox- focused server should condone flirting. This is a server for all ages and on the internet on a platform with less restrictions than Roblox, but designed around a topic targeted at underaged users, so I don't find it safe to allow romantic conversation, especially if they met here or in Roblox, because grooming is a thing, catfishing is a thing, predation is a thing. Now, I'm aware of the three besties who refer to each other as wifeys, and I've been disarmed about thuat, but I was initially not okay with it either. Close friend groups have the high potential to become uncomfortably toxic and exclusive from other members.As far as I can tell (as of last night when I wrote this), you're the only one who used a term typically related to someone's identity as a joke. You are not the only non-straight ...That’s understandable, with what you’re referring to in the first paragraph. I wish this was just said initially, I would have understood a lot sooner. Also, I was unaware the term wasn’t allowed, since Robot simply said I was muted since he thought I meant to say bitch. But I respect what you’re saying, now that I get where you’re coming from.

I see, so you didn’t know. I wasn’t sure. It didn’t make me necessarily uncomfortable, but it was… weird. I doubt they had bad intentions, but they were like, really obviously flirty, some borderline sexual jokes as well. That was mostly in game though, I wish I had screenshots from whenever we played this airplane game. That’s my bad. I didn’t want to report it because honestly, I dislike drama and arguments since I find in generally unnecessary That is, unless it goes against my personal values, I usually try not to start things. And I definitely do agree with what you’re saying in the second paragraph. As someone who was on discord way too young and has a lot of trauma from it, I know about grooming first hand. So I very much appreciate what you’re saying. Honestly, I think I was mostly just misled and confused. I feel a lot better hearing what you’re saying now.

It just initially felt like I was singled out since I wasn’t part of staff/ wasn’t part of the friend group. Also, since I’m new ish to the server/ didnt talk much until now.

I apologize if you thought my intention with making a joke about being a lesbian was romantic, because I would never. I have a girlfriend and that would just be weird, even if I wasn’t in a relationship. I’m just happy and comfortable with my identity to joke about it. I’ll be more mindful about what specific language I use. I’ve just been used to getting left out or pushed aside since I’m so open about it, that I got defensive and thought this was the case. I mean, it’s just because I’ve had friends leave me since I’m very unapologetically gay. Like the whole “it’s fine if you’re gay, just don’t push it in my face!!” was always surrounding me. Even in discord servers, where I’m just trying to make friends. Like yes, I really love sapphic media and culture… but I would never try to make people uncomfortable or imply that I want others to flirt with me. I would be super uncomfortable if that happened.

Overall, I hope this helps us see eye to eye. I know you said you aren’t on discord often anymore, so if you can share what I’m saying to the other staff, I’d appreciate it. I really don’t want people thinking I’m someone to dislike or find annoying/weird because of this whole situation.That’s understandable, with what you’re referring to in the first paragraph. I wish this was just said initially, I would have understood a lot sooner. Also, I was unaware the term...Yeah, I'm sorry Robot wasn't quite able to articulate our reasoning behind avoiding terms like butch. I just promoted him to co-owner in February, so he's still learning a lot about being the leader for our community in between learning offline at school, but I'll make sure he's well-equipped with all sorts of verbal arsenal and trained moderators that keep our community
Respectful
Inclusive
Cooperative, and
Honest.

It is never my wish for our staff to carry themselves in a hypocritical or unfair manner or push someone away for something that fits within our rules, guidelines, and values. And it is never our intention to ignore a problem, but to isolate and compartmentalize it. Talking about someone breaking a rule could be viewed as public shaming, and we don't want to do that either.

Thank you for understanding. I'll remove the warning, was there anything else or can I close this ticket?That should be everything. Feel free to close it, and thank you for speaking with me.Case #346 | Warn
